entry.title: Bitcoin covenants are inevitable
entry.summary: The metaphor of a "bottomless pit" is used to describe the irreversible nature of burning coins, highlighting that once these coins are burned, they cannot be utilized again in the future. This concept was brought up by Billy, who suggested that the frequent movement of coins might inherently yield utility. However, it is clarified that the true utility derived from burning coins comes exclusively from the proof associated with the process. Without such proof, the meaning and value of burning coins would remain ambiguous and unclear. This explanation serves to emphasize the importance of the proof in legitimizing the act of burning coins and ensuring its intended economic impact.
